sentences.sentence: How busy is too busy?
sentences.translation: 忙到什么程度才算过忙？

structure.text: How busy is too busy?

difficult_points: How busy 询问程度，too busy 是表语。
问题为全文讨论工作压力设定范围。

sentences.sentence: For some it means having to miss the occasional long lunch; for others it means missing lunch altogether.
sentences.translation: 对有些人而言，这意味着不得不错过偶尔的长午餐；对另一些人而言，则意味着完全没时间吃午餐。

structure.text: For some
it means having to miss the occasional long lunch
for others it means missing lunch altogether

difficult_points: 两个分号连接的分句对比不同人对“忙”的定义。
having to miss 与 missing 都是 mean 的动名词补语。

sentences.sentence: For a few, it is not being able to take a "sickie" once a month.
sentences.translation: 对少数人而言，则是连每月请一次“病假”都做不到。

structure.text: For a few
it is not being able to take a "sickie" once a month

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正内容是 not being able to...。
sickie 是口语化的病假说法，按引号保留。

expressions.text: be able to take a sickie

sentences.sentence: Then there is a group of people for whom working every evening and weekend is normal, and frantic is the tempo of their lives.
sentences.translation: 还有一群人，对他们而言每天晚上和周末工作都很正常，而他们的生活节奏却忙乱不堪。

structure.text: there is a group of people
for whom working every evening and weekend is normal
frantic is the tempo of their lives

difficult_points: for whom 引导定语从句，working 动名词短语作主语。
frantic is the tempo 是表语前置的倒装，正常语序是 the tempo...is frantic。

sentences.sentence: For most senior executives, workloads swing between extremely busy and frenzied.
sentences.translation: 对大多数高级管理人员来说，工作量在极其忙碌和疯狂忙乱之间摆动。

structure.text: For most senior executives
workloads
swing between extremely busy and frenzied

difficult_points: swing between...and... 表示在两种状态间变化。
extremely busy 与 frenzied 是工作量状态的并列表语。

sentences.sentence: The vice-president of the management consultancy AT Kearney and its head of telecommunications for the Asia-Pacific region, Neil Plumridge, says his work weeks vary from a "manageable" 45 hours to 80 hours, but average 60 hours.
sentences.translation: 管理咨询公司 AT Kearney 的副总裁兼亚太区电信业务负责人尼尔·普伦里奇说，他每周工作时间从“可承受的”45小时到80小时不等，但平均为60小时。

structure.text: The vice-president of the management consultancy AT Kearney and its head of telecommunications for the Asia-Pacific region, Neil Plumridge
says
his work weeks vary from a "manageable" 45 hours to 80 hours, but average 60 hours

difficult_points: 长主语包含两个职位和同位语 Neil Plumridge。
vary from...to... 与 average 两个谓语并列，主语都是 work weeks。

sentences.sentence: Three warning signs alert Plumridge about his workload: sleep, scheduling and family.
sentences.translation: 三个警示信号会提醒普伦里奇工作量过大：睡眠、日程安排和家庭。

structure.text: Three warning signs
alert Plumridge about his workload
sleep, scheduling and family

difficult_points: 冒号后的三个名词是 warning signs 的具体内容。
alert somebody about 表示提醒某人注意某事。

sentences.sentence: He knows he has too much on when he gets less than six hours of sleep for three consecutive nights; when he is constantly having to reschedule appointments; "and the third one is on the family side", says Plumridge, the father of a three-year-old daughter, and expecting a second child in October.
sentences.translation: 他知道自己事情太多，是因为连续三个晚上睡眠不足六小时、不得不不断改约，以及“第三个信号来自家庭方面”，普伦里奇说。他有一个三岁的女儿，并将在十月迎来第二个孩子。

structure.text: He knows
he has too much on when he gets less than six hours of sleep for three consecutive nights
when he is constantly having to reschedule appointments
"and the third one is on the family side"
the father of a three-year-old daughter, and expecting a second child in October

difficult_points: when 引导的几个条件并列说明工作过量的信号，分号和引号按原文保留。
the father... 是 Plumridge 的同位语，expecting 是补充说明。

sentences.sentence: "If I happen to miss a birthday or anniversary, I know things are out of control."Being "too busy" is highly subjective.
sentences.translation: “如果我碰巧错过生日或纪念日，我就知道事情已经失控了。”所谓“过忙”是非常主观的。

structure.text: If I happen to miss a birthday or anniversary
I know things are out of control
Being "too busy" is highly subjective

difficult_points: If 条件句中的 happen to 表示偶然发生。
源文引号结束后紧接 Being，无空格；按源文合并为一个条目。

sentences.sentence: But for any individual, the perception of being too busy over a prolonged period can start showing up as stress: disturbed sleep, and declining mental and physical health.
sentences.translation: 但对任何个人而言，长期觉得自己过于忙碌，可能开始表现为压力：睡眠紊乱以及身心健康下降。

structure.text: for any individual
the perception of being too busy over a prolonged period
can start showing up as stress
disturbed sleep, and declining mental and physical health

difficult_points: being too busy 是 perception of 的动名词补语。
show up as 表示以某种症状表现出来，冒号后列举具体表现。

sentences.sentence: National workers' compensation figures show stress causes the most lost time of any workplace injury.
sentences.translation: 全国工伤赔偿数据表明，压力造成的损失工作时间比任何其他工伤都多。

structure.text: National workers' compensation figures
show
stress causes the most lost time of any workplace injury

difficult_points: show 后省略 that，后面是宾语从句。
the most lost time 是最高级，of any workplace injury 表示比较范围。

sentences.sentence: Employees suffering stress are off work an average of 16.6 weeks.
sentences.translation: 承受压力的员工平均缺勤16.6周。

structure.text: Employees suffering stress
suffering stress
are off work an average of 16.6 weeks

difficult_points: suffering stress 是现在分词短语，修饰 Employees。
are off work 表示休假或缺勤，an average of 表示平均时长。

sentences.sentence: The effects of stress are also expensive.
sentences.translation: 压力的后果也代价高昂。

structure.text: The effects of stress
are also expensive

difficult_points: expensive 在这里指经济代价大，而非价格本身昂贵。
of stress 修饰 effects。

sentences.sentence: Comcare, the Federal Government insurer, reports that in 2003-04, claims for psychological injury accounted for 7% of claims but almost 27% of claim costs.
sentences.translation: 联邦政府保险机构 Comcare 报告称，2003至04年，心理伤害索赔占索赔总数的7%，却几乎占索赔成本的27%。

structure.text: Comcare, the Federal Government insurer
reports
that in 2003-04, claims for psychological injury accounted for 7% of claims but almost 27% of claim costs

difficult_points: 同位语说明 Comcare 的性质。
accounted for 两次并列，比较索赔数量比例和成本比例。

sentences.sentence: Experts say the key to dealing with stress is not to focus on relief – a game of golf or a massage – but to reassess workloads.
sentences.translation: 专家说，应对压力的关键不是把注意力放在缓解上——比如打高尔夫或按摩——而是重新评估工作量。

structure.text: Experts say
the key to dealing with stress is not to focus on relief – a game of golf or a massage – but to reassess workloads
a game of golf or a massage

difficult_points: the key to... is not...but... 构成否定对比。
破折号中的例子说明 relief 的具体方式。

sentences.sentence: Neil Plumridge says he makes it a priority to work out what has to change; that might mean allocating extra resources to a job, allowing more time or changing expectations.
sentences.translation: 尼尔·普伦里奇说，他把弄清必须改变什么作为优先事项；这可能意味着为工作分配额外资源、留出更多时间或改变预期。

structure.text: Neil Plumridge says
he makes it a priority to work out what has to change
what has to change
that might mean allocating extra resources to a job, allowing more time or changing expectations

difficult_points: make it a priority to do 中 it 是形式宾语，to work out 是真正宾语。
what has to change 是 work out 的宾语从句；三个动名词并列说明措施。

sentences.sentence: The decision may take several days.
sentences.translation: 作出决定可能需要几天。

structure.text: The decision
may take
several days

difficult_points: may 表示可能，take 表示花费时间。
The decision 指重新评估工作量后作出的调整决定。

sentences.sentence: He also relies on the advice of colleagues, saying his peers coach each other with business problems.
sentences.translation: 他还依靠同事的建议，并说同伴会互相指导处理业务问题。

structure.text: He also relies on the advice of colleagues
saying his peers coach each other with business problems

difficult_points: saying 分词短语补充说明他依靠同事建议的内容。
each other 是 coach 的反身相互代词。

sentences.sentence: "Just a fresh pair of eyes over an issue can help," he says.
sentences.translation: “只要换个角度审视问题就会有帮助，”他说。

structure.text: Just a fresh pair of eyes over an issue can help
he says

difficult_points: a fresh pair of eyes 是比喻，指新的观察角度。
引语后 he says 是倒装式引述结构。

sentences.sentence: Executive stress is not confined to big organisations.
sentences.translation: 高管压力并不只存在于大型组织。

structure.text: Executive stress
is not confined to big organisations

difficult_points: be confined to 表示局限于某范围。
not 强调高管压力也存在于小型企业。

sentences.sentence: Vanessa Stoykov has been running her own advertising and public relations business for seven years, specialising in work for financial and professional services firms.
sentences.translation: 瓦妮莎·斯托伊科夫经营自己的广告和公关公司已有七年，专门为金融和专业服务公司开展工作。

structure.text: Vanessa Stoykov
has been running her own advertising and public relations business for seven years
specialising in work for financial and professional services firms

difficult_points: has been running 是现在完成进行时，强调持续经营。
specialising 分词短语补充业务方向。

sentences.sentence: Evolution Media has grown so fast that it debuted on the BRW Fast 100 list of fastest-growing small enterprises last year – just after Stoykov had her first child.
sentences.translation: Evolution Media 发展如此迅速，去年登上了 BRW 快速成长百强小企业榜单——就在斯托伊科夫生下第一个孩子之后。

structure.text: Evolution Media has grown so fast
that it debuted on the BRW Fast 100 list of fastest-growing small enterprises last year
just after Stoykov had her first child

difficult_points: so...that... 表示发展速度之快带来的结果。
just after 引导时间从句，说明上榜与生子时间关系。

sentences.sentence: Stoykov thrives on the mental stimulation of running her own business.
sentences.translation: 斯托伊科夫享受经营自己企业所带来的脑力刺激。

structure.text: Stoykov
thrives on
the mental stimulation of running her own business

difficult_points: thrive on 表示从某事中获得活力和满足。
of running 是 stimulation 的动名词修饰。

sentences.sentence: "Like everyone, I have the occasional day when I think my head's going to blow off," she says.
sentences.translation: “和所有人一样，我偶尔也会觉得脑袋快要爆炸了，”她说。

structure.text: Like everyone
I have the occasional day
when I think my head's going to blow off
she says

difficult_points: when 从句修饰 day，描述压力达到极限的时刻。
head's going to blow off 是夸张的口语比喻。

sentences.sentence: Because of the growth phase the business is in, Stoykov has to concentrate on short-term stress relief –weekends in the mountains, the occasional "mental health" day – rather than delegating more work.
sentences.translation: 由于公司正处于成长阶段，斯托伊科夫不得不专注于短期压力缓解——去山里度周末、偶尔休一天“心理健康假”——而不是把更多工作委派出去。

structure.text: Because of the growth phase the business is in
Stoykov has to concentrate on short-term stress relief
rather than delegating more work

difficult_points: the business is in 是 phase 的定语从句，介词 in 后置。
rather than 后接动名词 delegating，与 concentrate on 的选择形成对比。

sentences.sentence: She says: "We're hiring more people, but you need to train them, teach them about the culture and the clients, so it's actually more work rather than less."
sentences.translation: 她说：“我们正在招聘更多人，但你需要培训他们，教他们了解企业文化和客户，所以实际上工作更多而不是更少。”

structure.text: We're hiring more people
but you need to train them, teach them about the culture and the clients
so it's actually more work rather than less

difficult_points: but 连接招聘与培训的转折；train 和 teach 并列。
so 引出结果，it 指培训新员工这件事。

sentences.sentence: Identify the causes: Jan Elsnera, Melbourne psychologist who specialises in executive coaching, says thriving on a demanding workload is typical of senior executives and other high-potential business people.
sentences.translation: 找出原因：墨尔本心理学家简·埃尔斯纳专门从事高管辅导，她说，享受高强度工作是高级管理人员和其他高潜力商业人士的典型特点。

structure.text: Identify the causes
Jan Elsnera, Melbourne psychologist who specialises in executive coaching
says thriving on a demanding workload is typical of senior executives and other high-potential business people
who specialises in executive coaching

difficult_points: 冒号后是引出专家观点的完整分句。
thriving on... 是 says 后宾语从句的动名词主语；who 修饰 psychologist。

sentences.sentence: She says there is no one-size-fits-all approach to stress: some people work best with high-adrenalin periods followed by quieter patches, while others thrive under sustained pressure.
sentences.translation: 她说，应对压力没有一刀切的方法：有些人在高肾上腺素阶段之后接一段安静期时表现最好，而另一些人在持续压力下反而能发挥得好。

structure.text: She says
there is no one-size-fits-all approach to stress
some people work best with high-adrenalin periods followed by quieter patches
while others thrive under sustained pressure

difficult_points: no one-size-fits-all 是“一刀切”的复合形容词。
followed by quieter patches 是过去分词短语，修饰 periods；while 引出对比人群。

sentences.sentence: "We could take urine and blood hormonal measures and pass a judgment of whether someone's physiologically stressed or not," she says.
sentences.translation: “我们可以测量尿液和血液中的激素，并判断某人在生理上是否承受压力，”她说。

structure.text: We could take urine and blood hormonal measures and pass a judgment
of whether someone's physiologically stressed or not
she says

difficult_points: could 表示一种可能的检测方式；take 和 pass 并列。
whether...or not 作 judgment of 的宾语，physiologically stressed 省略 be。

sentences.sentence: "But that's not going to give us an indicator of what their experience of stress is, and what the emotional and cognitive impacts of stress are going to be."
sentences.translation: “但这并不能告诉我们他们经历的压力是什么样，也不能告诉我们压力会造成怎样的情绪和认知影响。”

structure.text: that's not going to give us an indicator
of what their experience of stress is
and what the emotional and cognitive impacts of stress are going to be

difficult_points: be going to 表示预期结果，that 指前文的生理测量。
两个 what 从句并列作 indicator of 的内容。

sentences.sentence: Eisner's practice is informed by a movement known as positive psychology, a school of thought that argues "positive" experiences – feeling engaged, challenged, and that one is making a contribution to something meaningful – do not balance out negative ones such as stress; instead, they help people increase their resilience over time.
sentences.translation: 埃尔斯纳的实践受一种称为积极心理学的运动影响。这一思想流派认为，“积极”体验——感到投入、受到挑战并为有意义的事情作出贡献——并不能抵消压力等消极体验；相反，它们能帮助人们逐渐增强韧性。

structure.text: Eisner's practice is informed by a movement known as positive psychology
a school of thought that argues "positive" experiences – feeling engaged, challenged, and that one is making a contribution to something meaningful – do not balance out negative ones such as stress
"positive" experiences – feeling engaged, challenged, and that one is making a contribution to something meaningful – do not balance out negative ones such as stress
instead, they help people increase their resilience over time

difficult_points: known as positive psychology 是过去分词短语，修饰 movement。
that argues 后的长宾语从句包含破折号解释和 do not...instead 对比。
help + 宾语 + 动词原形表示帮助增强韧性。

sentences.sentence: Good stress, or positive experiences of being challenged and rewarded, is thus cumulative in the same way as bad stress.
sentences.translation: 因此，良性压力，即受到挑战和奖励的积极体验，会像不良压力一样累积。

structure.text: Good stress, or positive experiences of being challenged and rewarded
is thus cumulative
in the same way as bad stress

difficult_points: or 后是 good stress 的同位解释。
being challenged and rewarded 是 experiences of 的动名词被动结构。

sentences.sentence: Elsner says many of the senior business people she coaches are relying more on regulating bad stress through methods such as meditation and yoga.
sentences.translation: 埃尔斯纳说，她辅导的许多高级商业人士越来越依靠冥想和瑜伽等方法来调节不良压力。

structure.text: Elsner says
many of the senior business people she coaches are relying more on regulating bad stress
she coaches
through methods such as meditation and yoga

difficult_points: she coaches 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 people。
rely on 后接 regulating 动名词，through 短语说明方法。

sentences.sentence: She points to research showing that meditation can alter the biochemistry of the brain and actually help people"retrain" the way their brains and bodies react to stress.
sentences.translation: 她指出研究显示，冥想能够改变大脑的生化机制，实际上还能帮助人们“重新训练”大脑和身体对压力的反应方式。

structure.text: She points to research
showing that meditation can alter the biochemistry of the brain and actually help people"retrain" the way their brains and bodies react to stress
the way their brains and bodies react to stress

difficult_points: showing 分词短语修饰 research，that 从句中 alter 与 help 并列。
help people retrain 是使役结构；原文 people"retrain 缺空格，按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: "Meditation and yoga enable you to shift the way that your brain reacts, so if you get proficient at it you're in control."
sentences.translation: “冥想和瑜伽让你能够改变大脑的反应方式，所以如果你熟练掌握它们，就能掌控局面。”

structure.text: Meditation and yoga enable you to shift the way that your brain reacts
that your brain reacts
so if you get proficient at it you're in control
if you get proficient at it

difficult_points: enable + 宾语 + to do 表示使某人能够做某事。
so 与 if 分别引出结果和条件，it 指冥想和瑜伽。

sentences.sentence: The Australian vice-president of AT Kearney, Neil Plumridge, says: "Often stress is caused by our setting unrealistic expectations of ourselves. I'll promise a client I'll do something tomorrow, and then promise another client the same thing, when I really know it's not going to happen.I've put stress on myself when I could have said to the clients: 'Why don't I give that to you in 48 hours?' The client doesn't care." Over-committing is something people experience as an individual problem.
sentences.translation: AT Kearney 澳大利亚区副总裁尼尔·普伦里奇说：“压力往往源于我们对自己设定了不现实的期望。我会答应一个客户明天完成某事，然后又向另一个客户答应同样的事，尽管我明知做不到。我本可以对客户说：‘我48小时后给你怎么样？’但客户并不在意。”过度承诺是人们作为个人问题经历的事情。

structure.text: The Australian vice-president of AT Kearney, Neil Plumridge, says
Often stress is caused by our setting unrealistic expectations of ourselves
I'll promise a client I'll do something tomorrow, and then promise another client the same thing
when I really know it's not going to happen
I've put stress on myself when I could have said to the clients
Over-committing is something people experience as an individual problem

difficult_points: 源文引语中 happen.I've 无空格，且引语后接 Over-committing；为还原段落合并为一条。
be caused by 表示压力的原因，setting unrealistic expectations 是动名词短语。
could have said 表示本来可以采取但未采取的做法。

sentences.sentence: We explain it as the result of procrastination or Parkinson's law: that work expands to fill the time available.
sentences.translation: 我们把它解释为拖延或帕金森定律的结果：工作会扩展到填满可用时间。

structure.text: We explain it as the result of procrastination or Parkinson's law
that work expands to fill the time available
to fill the time available

difficult_points: explain + 宾语 + as 表示把某事解释为某原因。
冒号后的 that 从句解释 Parkinson's law；to fill 表示扩展的结果或目的。

sentences.sentence: New research indicates that people may be hard-wired to do it.
sentences.translation: 新研究表明，人们可能天生就会这样做。

structure.text: New research
indicates
that people may be hard-wired to do it

difficult_points: that 从句作 indicates 的宾语。
hard-wired 是比喻性的复合形容词，表示天生倾向；it 指过度承诺。

sentences.sentence: A study in the February issue of the Journal of Experimental Psychology shows that people always believe they will be less busy in the future than now.
sentences.translation: 《实验心理学杂志》二月刊的一项研究显示，人们总认为未来会比现在清闲。

structure.text: A study in the February issue of the Journal of Experimental Psychology
shows
that people always believe they will be less busy in the future than now
they will be less busy in the future than now

difficult_points: that 从句中又嵌入 believe 的宾语从句。
than now 省略了 than they are now，比较未来与现在的忙碌程度。

sentences.sentence: This is a misapprehension, according to the authors of the report, Professor Gal Zauberman, of the University of North Carolina, and Professor John Lynch, of Duke University.
sentences.translation: 报告作者、北卡罗来纳大学的加尔·扎伯曼教授和杜克大学的约翰·林奇教授认为，这是一种误解。

structure.text: This
is a misapprehension
according to the authors of the report
Professor Gal Zauberman, of the University of North Carolina, and Professor John Lynch, of Duke University

difficult_points: This 指前句认为未来更清闲的看法。
according to 短语引出观点来源，两个教授是 authors 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: "On average, an individual will be just as busy two weeks or a month from now as he or she is today.
sentences.translation: “平均而言，一个人两周或一个月后会和今天一样忙。

structure.text: On average
an individual will be just as busy two weeks or a month from now as he or she is today

difficult_points: as...as 比较结构表示未来与今天忙碌程度相同。
from now 表示从现在起的未来时间。

sentences.sentence: But that is not how it appears to be in everyday life," they wrote.
sentences.translation: 但在日常生活中，事情并不是看起来那样，”他们写道。

structure.text: But that is not how it appears to be in everyday life
they wrote

difficult_points: how it appears to be 是表语从句，说明事情呈现的样子。
that 指前句关于未来忙碌程度的判断。

sentences.sentence: "People often make commitments long in advance that they would never make if the same commitments required immediate action.
sentences.translation: “人们常常提前很久作出承诺，如果同样的承诺需要立即行动，他们绝不会作出。

structure.text: People often make commitments long in advance
that they would never make if the same commitments required immediate action
if the same commitments required immediate action

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 commitments。
if 从句使用过去式 required，表示假设条件；would never make 是假设结果。

sentences.sentence: That is, they discount future time investments relatively steeply." Why do we perceive a greater "surplus" of time in the future than in the present?
sentences.translation: 也就是说，他们会大幅折低对未来时间投入的估计。”为什么我们觉得未来比现在拥有更多“剩余”时间？

structure.text: That is
they discount future time investments relatively steeply
Why do we perceive a greater "surplus" of time in the future than in the present?

difficult_points: That is 用来换一种方式解释前句。
discount investments 表示低估未来投入；后面的疑问句转入作者提出的问题。

sentences.sentence: The researchers suggest that people underestimate completion times for tasks stretching into the future, and that they are bad at imagining future competition for their time.
sentences.translation: 研究人员认为，人们会低估延伸到未来的任务完成时间，也不善于想象未来对时间的竞争。

structure.text: The researchers suggest
that people underestimate completion times for tasks stretching into the future
and that they are bad at imagining future competition for their time
stretching into the future

difficult_points: 两个 that 从句并列作 suggest 的宾语。
stretching into the future 修饰 tasks；be bad at 后接动名词 imagining。
